records.ety: [OE. cripel, crepel, crupel, AS. crypel (akin to D. kreuple, G. krüppel, Dan. kröbling, Icel. kryppill), prop., one that can not walk, but must creep, fr. AS. crepan to creep. See Creep.]

defs: One who creeps, halts, or limps; one who has lost, or never had, the use of a limb or limbs; a lame person; hence, one who is partially disabled.
quotes: I am a cripple in my limbs; but what decays are in my mind, the reader must determine.

defs: Swampy or low wet ground, often covered with brush or with thickets; bog.
A rocky shallow in a stream; -- a lumberman's term.
quotes: The flats or cripple land lying between high- and low-water lines, and over which the waters of the stream ordinarily come and go.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Crippled (-p'ld); p. pr. & vb. n. Crippling (-pl?ng).]

defs: To deprive of the use of a limb, particularly of a leg or foot; to lame.
quotes: He had crippled the joints of the noble child.

defs: To deprive of strength, activity, or capability for service or use; to disable; to deprive of resources; as, to be financially crippled.
quotes: More serious embarrassments . . . were crippling the energy of the settlement in the Bay.
An incumbrance which would permanently cripple the body politic.
